title: Intermodal Container Dwell Time
spl:
index=logistics sourcetype="container:dwell"
| where isnotnull(dwell_hours)
| stats avg(dwell_hours) as avg_dwell, perc95(dwell_hours) as p95_dwell, max(dwell_hours) as max_dwell by facility
| sort - p95_dwell
| table facility, avg_dwell, p95_dwell, max_dwell
description: Excessive container dwell time at terminals increases demurrage costs and reduces asset turns. Tracking dwell enables process improvements and better planning.
value: Excessive container dwell time at terminals increases demurrage costs and reduces asset turns. Tracking dwell enables process improvements and better planning.
implementation: Define dwell from ingate to outgate. Compare rail vs truck facilities. Target reduction projects at high-p95 sites. **Domain context:** Demurrage/detention charges incentivize dwell reduction; intermodal metrics often feed into shipper scorecards (OTIF). **Splunk:** If `dwell_hours` is snapshot, not event-derived, confirm refresh cadence to avoid stale rankings.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Terminal operating system via HEC.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="container:dwell"` fields `container_id`, `dwell_hours`, `facility`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Define dwell from ingate to outgate. Compare rail vs truck facilities. Target reduction projects at high-p95 sites. **Domain context:** Demurrage/detention charges incentivize dwell reduction; intermodal metrics often feed into shipper scorecards (OTIF). **Splunk:** If `dwell_hours` is snapshot, not event-derived, confirm refresh cadence to avoid stale rankings.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="container:dwell"
| where isnotnull(dwell_hours)
| stats avg(dwell_hours) as avg_dwell, perc95(dwell_hours) as p95_dwell, max(dwell_hours) as max_dwell by facility
| sort - p95_dwell
| table facility, avg_dwell, p95_dwell, max_dwell
```

Understanding this SPL

**Intermodal Container Dwell Time** ‚Äî Excessive container dwell time at terminals increases demurrage costs and reduces asset turns. Tracking dwell enables process improvements and better planning.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=logistics` `sourcetype="container:dwell"` fields `container_id`, `dwell_hours`, `facility`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Terminal operating system via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: logistics; **sourcetype**: container:dwell. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=logistics, sourcetype="container:dwell". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(dwell_hours)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by facility** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Intermodal Container Dwell Time**): table facility, avg_dwell, p95_dwell, max_dwell


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (p95 by facility), Histogram (dwell distribution), Trend of avg dwell.
visualization: Bar chart (p95 by facility), Histogram (dwell distribution), Trend of avg dwell.
